title: Man Utd make Osimhen top target - Wednesday's gossip
content:
Express back page

Guardian back page

Mail back page

Star back page

Telegraph back page

The i back page

The Times back page

1 of 7

Manchester United make Victor Osimhen their number one striking target but face competition for Matheus Cunha, while Aston Villa look set to win the race for 18-year-old Sverre Nypan.

Manchester Unitedhave madeNapoli's26-year-old Nigeria striker Victor Osimhen,  currently on loan atGalatasaray, their top striking transfer target this summer.(Mirror),external

Manchester Unitedare hoping to pay Matheus Cunha's Â£62.5m release clause in instalments, withWolvesopen to splitting the payments for the 25-year-old Brazil forward.(Telegraph),external

Arsenal,NewcastleandNottingham Foresthave all registered an interest in Cunha.(Talksport),external

AstonVillaare set to beat Arsenal andManchester Cityto the signing ofRosenborg's18-year-old Norway Under-21s midfielder Sverre Nypan.(Times - subscription required),external

Manchester Unitedalso plan to trigger the Â£30m release clause in the contract ofIpswich's22-year-old English striker Liam Delap.(Mail - subscription required),external

Ange Postecoglou is likely to leaveTottenhameven if they win the Europa League and qualify for next season's Champions League.(Telegraph - subscription required),external

Listen to the latest Football Daily podcast

Follow your club with BBC Sport

Bournemouthboss Andoni Iraola,Fulham'sMarco Silva and Scott Parker ofBurnleyare potential candidates to succeed Postecoglou.(Mail),external

BayerLeverkusenhave held talks withManchesterCityover a deal for 22-year-old English forward James McAtee.(Florian Plettenberg),external

Lee Carsley, Danny Rohl and Russell Martin are onLeicester'sshortlist should they sack manager Ruud van Nistelrooy.(Sky Sports),external

Chelseahave joined the race forPortogoalkeeper Diogo Costa, 25, and will challengeManchester Unitedfor the signature of the Portugal international.(Teamtalk),external

ManchesterCityare also eyeing Costa as a replacement for Brazil goalkeeper Ederson, 31, who is in talks with clubs in Saudi Arabia.(Florian Plettenberg),external

Newcastleare interested inStrasbourg's22-year-old Dutch forward Emanuel Emegha.(L'Equipe - in French, subscription required),external

Evertonare interested inFeyenoord'sSlovakia centre-back David Hancko, 27, but face competition fromJuventus, Atletico MadridandBayerLeverkusen.(Tuttomercatoweb - in Italian),external

West Hamand Ghana winger Mohammed Kudus, 24, is a top target for Saudi Pro League clubAl-Nassr.(Football Insider),external

Saudi Arabian clubAl-Hilalare interested inBarcelona's28-year-old Brazil forward Raphinha.(Sport - in Spanish),external

Nottingham Foresthave set their sights on Scottish defender Dara Jikiemi, 15, who has caught the eye inCeltic'sacademy.(Football Insider),external

Manchester City, LiverpoolandArsenalare eyeingBayerLeverkusen's21-year-old Germany attacking midfielder Florian Wirtz, who is valued at about Â£103m.(CaughtOffside),external

Bournemouthand Spaindefender Dean Huijsen, 20, is one of the top targets forReal Madridthis summer.(AS - in Spanish,external)

Tottenhamcould move for winger Marcus Rashford, 27, this summer ifAston Villadecide against making his loan move fromManchester Unitedpermanent.(Football Transfers,external)

Manchester Unitedare plotting moves forNewcastle'sBruno Guimaraes, 27, andCrystal Palace'sAdam Wharton, 21, to bolster their midfield.(TeamTalk,external)
